methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $33M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QunaSys is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $33M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identifiable peers: 3 public entities (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private companies including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and others at varying stages.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $33M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage transitions, yielding $63M. Peer-median comparison is not applicable due to insufficient narrow comparables—while 7 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 companies satisfy simultaneous filters for stage, adjacency, or valuation tier, making sector-wide median estimates unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $72M (high), centered on $63M. This range reflects the single applicable triangulation method and incorporates ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage peer data, limiting confidence in step-up multiples derived from broader market patterns, and the early-stage nature of quantum computing commercialization, which constrains historical comparability. Further valuation refinement would require additional Series B or Series C quantum computing transactions or disclosure of QunaSys-specific operating metrics.
